claim: Eval maturity model: start with vibe checks using documented human justifications (not just thumbs up/down) before scaling to LLM judges.
actionable: Begin your eval program by collecting human annotations with explicit reasoning; these justifications become the training ground for automated scoring later.
source_title: The maturity phases of running evals — Phil Hetzel, Braintrust

claim: Evals should focus on known failure modes, not exhaustive coverage; shipping with targeted evals beats comprehensive vibe checks.
actionable: Identify 3-5 critical failure modes for your agent, build evals specifically for those, and ship; let production traces reveal new gaps.
source_title: The maturity phases of running evals — Phil Hetzel, Braintrust

claim: The eval flywheel: capture production traces → understand failures → offline experimentation → guide improvements → capture new traces.
actionable: Set up logging in production; when agents fail, bring those traces into your eval dataset, fix the issue, then verify with offline tests.
source_title: The maturity phases of running evals — Phil Hetzel, Braintrust

claim: Context-gathering tools are manageable in evals; CRUD tools (create/read/update/delete external systems) are hard because you must represent system state at eval-time.
actionable: For CRUD-heavy agents, inject captured system state directly into traces at the moment of the original action or use timestamp queries against version-controlled data.
source_title: The maturity phases of running evals — Phil Hetzel, Braintrust

claim: Eval LLM judges should themselves be evaluated; deterministic graders are not always better—embrace LLM judges but validate them against ground truth.
actionable: Create a ground-truth dataset for your LLM judge outputs; continuously validate that the judge aligns with human decisions.
source_title: The maturity phases of running evals — Phil Hetzel, Braintrust

claim: Eval results don't need to be perfect—directional trends are sufficient when using non-deterministic techniques like LLM-as-judge.
actionable: Track eval results over time; focus on the trend direction rather than absolute accuracy when using probabilistic scoring functions.
source_title: The maturity phases of running evals — Phil Hetzel, Braintrust
